title: Polarizable Embedding QM/MM for Periodic Systems
authors: Julian Bessner, Anoop Ajaya Kumar Nair, Magnus Andreas Hilduberg Christiansen, Timo Jacob, Hannes Jónsson, Elvar Örn Jónsson
abstract: A general polarizable embedded (PE) quantum mechanics/molecular mechanics scheme for periodic systems is presented, describing mutual polarization of the two subsystems. The QM system, described with density functional theory (DFT), is coupled to a single center multipole expansion (SCME) model, characterising H$_2$O molecules in the MM region. In SCME the H$_2$O molecules are ascribed anisotropic dipole and quadrupole polarizabilities and permanent multipoles up to and including the hexadecapole. Our embedding scheme illustrates a smooth and efficient convergence pattern of the periodic interaction potential by introducing a single and clustered multipole expansion points in the far-field. By choosing the near- and far-field expansion of the potential carefully the PE-QM/MM calculation matches the level of accuracy of a the QM calculation. In the short range, the electrostatic interaction between the QM and MM subsystems is damped with a real-space and pair-wise isotropic damping functions - resulting in a screened interaction and preventing over-polarization. In molecular dynamics simulations the two subsystems are separated with the elastic scattering assisted flexible inner region [Kirchhoff et. al. JCTC, 2021, 17, 9, 5863] - ensuring a smooth transition in the radial distribution at the boundary between the two subsystems.
summary: The paper presents a new numerical method for simulating periodic systems using a polarizable QM/MM approach. It improves accuracy and convergence by using a sophisticated multipole expansion and damping functions to handle the interface between quantum and classical regions. This allows for efficient large-scale simulations of complex molecular environments.
main_problem: Developing an efficient and accurate polarizable embedding QM/MM scheme for periodic systems that accounts for mutual polarization between the QM and MM regions.
main_result: The presented scheme achieves a smooth and efficient convergence of the periodic interaction potential and matches the accuracy of full QM calculations by carefully choosing near- and far-field expansions.
method: A polarizable embedded (PE) QM/MM scheme using Density Functional Theory (DFT) for the QM region and a single center multipole expansion (SCME) model for the MM region, incorporating anisotropic polarizabilities and isotropic damping functions.
model_or_system: A periodic system consisting of a QM subsystem coupled to an MM region containing H2O molecules characterized by permanent multipoles up to the hexadecapole and anisotropic dipole/quadrupole polarizabilities.
key_observables: periodic interaction potential, radial distribution at the boundary, electrostatic interaction energy
important_parameters: multipole expansion orders (up to hexadecapole), damping functions for short-range interactions
main_assumptions: The MM region is represented by a single center multipole expansion (SCME) model; the QM region is described via DFT.
paper_structure: The paper introduces a new PE-QM/MM scheme for periodic systems, details the SCME model for water molecules, describes the implementation of near- and far-field expansions and damping functions, and discusses the application to molecular dynamics simulations.
analysis_basis: abstract only; PDF text extraction failed or PDF unavailable
